steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question tests Command of Evidence (Conclusions). The passage establishes three facts: (1) silicon alone converts 18–22% of sunlight; (2) perovskite alone is no more efficient than silicon; (3) silicon + perovskite together converts 29.5%+. The decisive clue is that perovskite captures blue-range light and silicon captures red-range light — together they cover more of the spectrum. The blank must explain why the combination outperforms either material alone.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘photovoltaic cells with both silicon and perovskite are more efficient because they make use of more of the solar spectrum.’: Directly follows from the complementary wavelength coverage (blue + red) and the observed 29.5%+ combined efficiency — neither material achieves this alone. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘photovoltaic cells with only perovskite and no silicon would likely convert more than 29.5% of the Sun’s energy.’: Directly contradicted by the passage, which states perovskite-only cells are no more efficient than silicon-based cells (18–22%). ✗
  • Choice C — ‘solar power will remain elusive until photovoltaic cells are replaced with a more practical technology.’: The passage describes an improvement to existing cells, not their replacement, and never suggests solar power is elusive. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘researchers need to evaluate whether other minerals like perovskite are as effective as perovskite seems to be.’: The passage focuses solely on the perovskite-silicon combination; no other minerals are mentioned or implied as a next research step. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
